name: column
description: Format text into aligned columns or a table. Reads from standard input and arranges text into neatly spaced columns. Use -t to create a table from delimited input (whitespace by default), -s to specify a custom input delimiter, -x to fill columns before rows. Use for pretty-printing tabular data, aligning whitespace-separated fields, or formatting command output into readable tables.

input.description: Text to format, provided on standard input. The first argument is flags (e.g. '-t', '-t -s,').
